strategies.summary: Multiple feature pairs exhibit near-perfect correlation (≥0.9993), indicating strong linear dependencies and potential redundancy among packet length, segment size, flag counts, and packet count features, which may reduce effective feature dimensionality.
strategies.strategic_objective: Clarify stability and class-dependence of strong feature correlations and assess redundancy impact on model simplification potential.

strategies.summary: Destination Port feature shows strong distributional divergence between BENIGN and DDoS classes, with the DDoS class dominated by a single port and zero entropy, suggesting a representation-sensitive signal potentially linked to attack targeting.
strategies.strategic_objective: Determine temporal stability and sampling sensitivity of the dominant port distribution in the DDoS class to assess representation sensitivity.

strategies.summary: Features with near-constant values (low diversity) coexist with features showing strong class-conditioned structural variation, creating tension between stable and variable feature subsets that complicates unified interpretation of dataset structure.
strategies.strategic_objective: Explore mechanisms behind coexistence of near-constant and strongly class-variable features and implications for model reliance balancing.
